data.title: As a DSO agent, planning skills record tokens_estimated on the parent epic/story
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend `check_usage.py` to expose per-session token delta (not just bucket percentages). Planning skills (brainstorm, preplanning, implementation-plan) call this at their own start and end to compute tokens_estimated and write it to the parent epic/story.

**Why**: Planning-phase tokens are the "overhead" of the sprint — separating them from execution tokens lets users see planning-cost-vs-execution-cost ratio. Currently `check_usage.py` returns only rolled-up 5hr/7day percentages; it needs a session-delta mode.

**Scope**:
- IN: Add a `--session-delta` mode to `check_usage.py` that returns tokens used by the current session only.
- IN: Brainstorm, preplanning, implementation-plan invoke it at skill entry and exit to compute their own token spend.
- IN: Write the delta to the parent epic/story via `ticket-edit.sh --tokens_estimated=` (additive on existing value).
- OUT: Retroactive estimation.
- OUT: Measurement of execution tokens — separate story.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running `/dso:brainstorm` on an epic increments the epic ticket tokens_estimated by the skill session token delta.
  ← Satisfies: "Estimate planning token use on the parent ticket"
- When this story is complete, the same behavior applies for `/dso:preplanning` and `/dso:implementation-plan`.
  ← Satisfies: "Estimate planning token use on the parent ticket"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Reliability] If `check_usage.py` session-delta is unavailable (e.g., offline), skip the write silently rather than blocking skill execution.
- [Maintainability] The planning-skill integration is a single source line per skill — handle it in a shared helper so the pattern is DRY.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
